title: Replace Agent tool with dedicated subagent_resume
description: Remove the Agent tool and create a focused subagent_resume tool for session-mode resumption

work_packages.title: Create subagent_resume tool and remove Agent
work_packages.description: Implement the new subagent_resume tool, delete the Agent tool, update registration, suppression list, and system prompt references. T003 and T004 are internally parallelizable after T002 completes.
work_packages.rationale: All tasks touch the same tool registration surface and must be coordinated — the new tool replaces the old one in one atomic change.

work_packages.title: Migrate tests
work_packages.description: Update all test files that reference Agent or registerAgentTool to use the new subagent_resume tool and redirect behavioral tests to surviving tools.
work_packages.rationale: Tests depend on the production code changes from WP1. Split into rename-style migrations (P2-T001) and behavioral test redirection (P2-T002) that share the same test infrastructure.

phases.name: Tool implementation and cleanup
phases.description: Create the new subagent_resume tool, remove the Agent tool, and update all production references

tasks.title: Create subagent_resume tool
tasks.description: Implement `src/tools/resume.ts` with a `subagent_resume` tool that takes `{ agent_id, prompt }` parameters plus optional provider, model, and thinking overrides. Provide session-mode agent resumption: acquire concurrency, consume completion, resume the handle, return formatted state or error. Follow the registration pattern in `src/tools/steer.ts`.
acceptance_criteria: Tool registered with name 'subagent_resume' and parameters agent_id (string, required), prompt (string, required), plus optional provider, model, and thinking override parameters
Returns an active-limit-reached error when concurrency is exhausted
Completion notification for the resumed agent is consumed so it is not re-delivered to the parent
Returns formatted state on success including agentId, alias, status, finalOutput, and usage
Returns descriptive error when agent not found or not session-mode
registerResumeTool is imported and called in src/index.ts alongside other tool registrations
Includes renderResult handler delegating to renderDispatchResult for consistent UI rendering
tasks.context: REQ-1 — the resume capability is the only unique functionality of the Agent tool that needs preservation

tasks.title: Remove Agent tool and update registration
tasks.description: Delete `src/tools/agent.ts`. Remove the `registerAgentTool` import and its call site from `src/index.ts`. Ensure no other production file imports from `./tools/agent.js`.
acceptance_criteria: src/tools/agent.ts does not exist
src/index.ts has no import of registerAgentTool
No production source file references ./tools/agent or registerAgentTool
TypeScript compilation succeeds after removal
tasks.context: REQ-2 — Agent is fully redundant once subagent_resume exists

tasks.title: Update tool suppression list
tasks.description: In `src/runtime/tool-suppression.ts`, replace `"Agent"` with `"subagent_resume"` in the `PI_CREW_ORCHESTRATION_TOOL_NAMES` array.
acceptance_criteria: PI_CREW_ORCHESTRATION_TOOL_NAMES includes 'subagent_resume'
PI_CREW_ORCHESTRATION_TOOL_NAMES does not include 'Agent'
Tool suppression logic still functions for sub-agent sessions
tasks.context: REQ-4 — suppression prevents sub-agents from recursively launching orchestration tools

tasks.title: Update system prompt
tasks.description: In `src/system-prompt.ts`, remove references to `Agent` in the dispatch model section. Add `subagent_resume` to the tracking section alongside `steer_subagent`. Remove the Claude-style line about `Agent` from the dispatch model bullets.
acceptance_criteria: System prompt no longer mentions 'Agent' as a tool name
System prompt documents subagent_resume for session-mode resumption
Dispatch model section describes only subagent_dispatch and subagent_run for launching
tasks.context: REQ-3 — the LLM system prompt must accurately reflect the available tools

phases.name: Test migration
phases.description: Update all tests referencing Agent to validate the new tool surface

tasks.title: Migrate straightforward Agent test references
tasks.description: Update `test/unit/tool-schema.test.ts` to test `subagent_resume` schema properties (agent_id, prompt, model, alias required). Update `test/unit/session-lifecycle.test.ts` to replace `'Agent'` with `'subagent_resume'` in tool name lists and suppression assertions.
acceptance_criteria: tool-schema.test.ts validates subagent_resume has agent_id, prompt, model, and alias properties
session-lifecycle.test.ts tool name lists include subagent_resume instead of Agent
Resume behavior is tested in at least one test case
tasks.context: REQ-5 — straightforward renames where Agent was referenced by name only

tasks.title: Migrate explore-blocking and detach behavior tests
tasks.description: Redirect `test/unit/explore-blocking.test.ts` to test explore-blocking coercion against `subagent_dispatch` (which owns this behavior post-removal). Redirect `test/unit/detach.test.ts` Agent-tool tests to exercise detach behavior via `subagent_run` (which handles the detach/background race). Remove tests that duplicate coverage already provided by dispatch/run test suites.
acceptance_criteria: Explore-blocking coercion is tested against subagent_dispatch and/or subagent_run
Detach-to-background behavior is tested against subagent_run
No test file imports from src/tools/agent.js or references registerAgentTool
npm test passes with zero failures
tasks.context: REQ-5 + AC-6 — these tests exercise dispatch/detach behavior that lived in Agent but now belongs to subagent_dispatch and subagent_run
